title: Clarification on --public-flat-rw
body:
If I understand correctly, `--public-flat--rw` will make writes to any variable/wire propagate appropriately. But see the wave I got 

### Test
```systemverilog
module t();

    logic a;
    logic b;

    assign b=a;

endmodule
```
### Wave
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48233402/72277385-d3b30180-3657-11ea-8134-4403e5a47990.png)


### Question

 1. Is this a bug, or am I missing something?
 2. Is it possible make an interface instance public_rw by something like  `Bus b1 () /*verilator public_flat_rw @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_clk) */ ;`
